title: MCP Installation
subtitle: Connect AI IDEs to Todo for AI with MCP

overview.title: MCP Feature Overview
overview.description: Todo for AI's MCP (Model Context Protocol) server allows AI assistants to directly access and manage your tasks. Through MCP, AI can read task information, create new tasks, update progress, enabling true intelligent task management.

whatIsMcp.title: What is MCP?
whatIsMcp.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open protocol launched by Anthropic that allows AI assistants to securely access external tools and data sources. Through MCP, AI can directly interact with your Todo for AI account, enabling:
features: Context-aware intelligent suggestions based on project context

supportedTools.title: Supported Tools (6)
supportedTools.getProjectTasks: Get pending tasks by project name with status filtering
supportedTools.getTaskById: Get detailed task information by ID, including project context rules
supportedTools.createTask: Create new tasks in specified projects with priority, due date, etc.
supportedTools.submitFeedback: Submit feedback for tasks and update status (in_progress/review/done)
supportedTools.getProjectInfo: Get detailed project information including statistics, recent tasks
supportedTools.listUserProjects: List all projects accessible to the user with optional statistics

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.backend: Todo for AI backend service accessible
requirements.ide: AI IDE with MCP protocol support (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.)

alert.description: Todo for AI's MCP integration uses the @todo-for-ai/mcp npm package. Simply configure the JSON to use it.

description.suffix: page to create your dedicated Token. This Token is used to authenticate MCP clients.

second.title: Step 2: Copy MCP Configuration
second.description: Based on your AI client, copy the configuration to the appropriate location:

third.title: Step 3: Verify Configuration
third.description: After configuration, verify that MCP is working properly:

items.restart: Completely close and restart your AI client
items.testCommand: Enter test commands in conversation, such as 'Get my project list'
items.verify: Check if AI can return project or task information correctly

claude.title: Claude Desktop Configuration
claude.description: Claude Desktop is the official desktop application provided by Anthropic with native MCP support.

editConfig.title: Edit Configuration
editConfig.description: Open the configuration file and add Todo for AI configuration in the mcpServers section:

restart.title: Restart Claude Desktop
restart.description: After saving the configuration file, completely quit Claude Desktop and reopen it. The MCP server will be automatically downloaded and installed on first launch.

verify.title: Verify Installation
verify.description: In Claude conversation, you should see a hammer icon (Tools). Click it to see available Todo for AI tools.

cursor.title: Cursor IDE Configuration
cursor.description: Cursor is an AI-powered code editor based on VS Code with MCP protocol support.

openSettings.title: Open Settings
openSettings.description: Click the settings icon (⚙️) in the lower left corner -> MCP, or press Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + P and search for 'MCP'.

addServer.title: Add MCP Server
addServer.description: Click the 'Add MCP Server' button and fill in as follows:
addServer.name: Name: todo-for-ai

save.title: Save Configuration
save.description: After saving, Cursor will automatically start the MCP server. If the status shows green, the configuration is successful.

useInChat.title: Use in Chat
useInChat.description: In Cursor's AI conversation, MCP tools will be automatically called when needed. You can also directly ask about task-related information.

otherIdes.title: Other MCP-Compatible IDEs
otherIdes.description: In addition to Claude Desktop and Cursor, many tools support the MCP protocol:

windsurf.title: Windsurf
windsurf.description: AI IDE launched by Codeium. Search for MCP in settings to configure. Similar configuration method to Cursor.

cline.title: Cline (VS Code Extension)
cline.description: VS Code AI programming assistant extension with MCP tool support. Add MCP server configuration in Cline settings.

continue.title: Continue (VS Code Extension)
continue.description: Open source AI programming assistant. Add MCP server configuration in config.json.

genericConfig.title: Generic Configuration Format
genericConfig.description: Most MCP clients use the following JSON format:

configuration.title: Configuration Parameters
configuration.description: The MCP server supports the following configuration parameters:

apiBaseUrl.name: --api-base-url
apiBaseUrl.description: Todo for AI API base URL

logLevel.name: --log-level
logLevel.description: Log level, options: debug, info, warn, error

envVars.title: Environment Variable Configuration
envVars.description: You can also use environment variables instead of command line parameters:

security.title: Security Tips
tips: API Token is like your password, do not share it with others
Do not commit configuration files with real Tokens to code repositories
If you suspect Token leakage, delete and recreate it immediately

testing.title: Testing & Verification
testing.description: After configuration, use the following methods to test if MCP is working properly:

testCommands.title: Test Command Examples
testCommands.listTasks: Get pending tasks for project [project name]
testCommands.createTask: Create a task in project [project name] with title [task title]

errors.solution: Ensure Node.js is properly installed and npm global bin directory is in PATH
Check if API Token is correct, expired, or deleted

errors.symptom: MCP server starts but no response
errors.solution: Check log output, use --log-level debug for detailed logs

errors.symptom: Cannot see tool icon in Claude
errors.solution: Ensure configuration file format is correct, restart Claude Desktop, wait for auto-installation to complete

support.title: Get Help
support.description: If you still have issues, get help through the following channels:
